item.title: 最大号在数组中使用递归 | Largest no. in Array using Recursion
item.description:
大家好，今天我想向您展示如何使用递归查找数组中的最大数。 好吧，我今天发现了这个问题，然后搜索了stackoverflow😂并找到了这个解决方案，然后试图理解这个解决方案。

让我们开始吧...

代码 -

 const findMax = arr => { if ( ! Array . isArray ( arr )) throw ' Not an array ' if ( arr . length === 0 ) return undefined const [ head , ... tail ] = arr if ( arr . length === 1 ) return head return head > findMax ( tail ) ? head : findMax ( tail ) } console . log ( findMax ([ 1 , 9 , 10 , 28 , 78 , 10 ]))

在职的 -

  • 首先我们将检查传递的参数是否是箭头，如果它不是数组则抛出错误
  • 然后我们将检查数组的长度是否为 0，如果是，则返回 undefined。
  • 在下一行中，我们将数组解构为两部分 - head 和 tail，head 包含数组的第一个元素，...tail 包含数组的其余部分
  • 之后，我们检查数组长度是否为 1 ，如果是，那么我们将返回头部（该数组的第一个也是唯一的元素）
  • 然后我们使用三元运算符来提供条件，如果 head 大于 findMax(tail) 意味着 head 元素是第一个元素， findMax(tail) 将给出 tail 数组中的第一个元素，它是之后的下一个元素那，所以我们将比较这两个，如果条件为真，我们将返回最大的头元素，如果条件为假，则我们将递归检查尾部并将该数组的第一个元素用作头部，其余的作为其他尾部元素
  • 因此，它会递归地检查和比较数组中的所有元素，并为我们提供最大的元素。

它看起来很混乱，我尽力解释，如果您发现任何错误，请在评论部分更正。

感谢您查看这篇文章^^您可以通过以下链接捐款帮助我谢谢👇👇 ^^

还要检查这些帖子
艾米对此的 Vue - 库尔迪普·塔拉帕拉 - 一旦暂停，shubhamtiwari909 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，shubhamtiwari909 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，shubhamtiwari909 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 shubhamtiwari909 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 shubhamtiwari909 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 shubhamtiwari909 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hello guys today i want to show you how to find largest number in array using recursion. Well i found this question today and then searched the stackoverflow😂 and found this solution and then tried to understand this solution.

Lets get started...

Code - 

const findMax = arr => { if (!Array.isArray(arr)) throw 'Not an array' if (arr.length === 0) return undefined const [head, ...tail] = arr if (arr.length === 1) return head return head > findMax(tail) ? head : findMax(tail)
} console.log(findMax([1,9,10,28,78,10]))

Working -

  • First we will check that the argument passed is an arrow or not , if it is not an array then throw an error
  • Then we will check if the length of array is 0 , if it is , return undefined.
  • In the next line , we destructured the array in two parts - head and tail, head containes the first element of the array and ...tail contains the rest of the array
  • After that we check that if the array length is 1 , if it is then we will return the head (the first and only element of that array)
  • Then we used the ternary operator to provide the condition, if the head is greater than findMax(tail) meaning the head element is the first element and findMax(tail) will give the first element from the tail array which is the next element jsut after that , so we will compare those two , if the condition is true , we will return the head element as the largest and if the condition is false then we will recursively check the tail and use the first element of that array as head and the rest as other tail element
  • So, Recursively it will check and compare all the elements in the array and gives us the largest one.

It looks quite confusing and i tried my best to explain and if you find any point wrong please correct it in the comment section.

THANK YOU FOR CHECKING THIS POST ^^You can help me by some donation at the link below Thank you👇👇 ^^

https://dev.to/shubhamtiwari909/e-quotes-3bng

https://dev.to/shubhamtiwari909/deploy-react-app-on-netlify-kl

Amy's Vue on this - Jul 25

Kuldeep Tarapara - Jul 28

item.title: DynamoDB 的优点和缺点 | DynamoDB Pros & Cons
item.description:
它以无缝的可扩展性提供快速且可预测的性能。 它还可以在任何规模上提供一致的性能和可用性。

表现

与传统关系数据库相比，DynamoDB 的主要优势之一是其性能。 它使用独特的分区方案，允许它在多个服务器之间分配数据，同时保持低延迟和高吞吐量。

容量

可扩展性

它还通过向单个服务器添加更多容量来垂直扩展。 这使 DynamoDB 能够在不牺牲性能或可用性的情况下处理突然的流量高峰。

可用性

它使用多个可用区 (AZ) 在不同地理区域的多个服务器之间复制数据。 这可确保您的数据始终可用，即使一个或多个可用区出现故障。

容错

如果主副本不可用，DynamoDB 会自动故障转移到另一个 AZ 中的备用副本。

完全托管

这包括修补、备份、监控和扩展数据库。 这可以节省您的时间，以便您可以专注于开发应用程序，而不必担心管理基础设施。

安全性和合规性

审计

您还可以使用 AWS CloudTrail 记录所有 DynamoDB API 调用以进行进一步审计。

虽然 DynamoDB 具有许多优点，例如高度可扩展和提供低延迟，但使用此服务也有一些缺点。

大规模昂贵

这样做的原因是 DynamoDB 是一项托管服务，这意味着 AWS 会为您处理所有底层基础设施和管理。 如果您不熟悉 AWS 或者您不想担心管理自己的数据库，这很好，但这是有代价的。 当您需要扩展 DynamoDB 时，您实际上是在为 AWS 支付费用来为您完成所有工作，这可以快速增加。

对于需要关系数据库结构的工作负载，DynamoDB 不是一个好的选择。 这是因为 DynamoDB 是 NoSQL 数据库，这意味着它不支持关系数据库模型。 此外，DynamoDB 不支持外键，这是在关系数据库中定义表之间的关系所必需的。 因此，DynamoDB 不适合需要复杂关系数据库结构的工作负载。

数据一致性

这使得它不适合需要高度数据一致性的工作负载，例如金融交易。

复杂查询

例如，需要全表扫描的查询可能非常慢。 此外，DynamoDB 不支持索引，因此很难在大表中找到特定项目。

总之，对于需要强大数据库解决方案的人来说，DynamoDB 是一个很好的工具。 然而，它也有它的缺点。 如果您正在寻找轻松而诙谐的 DynamoDB 优缺点，那么这篇文章就是为您准备的！

为我们的Github存储库加注星标并加入我们的Discord 频道中的讨论，以帮助我们使 BLST 变得更好！
现在在BLST免费测试您的 API！



原文:

AWS DynamoDB is a fast, scalable, and fully managed NoSQL database service. It provides fast and predictable performance with seamless scalability. DynamoDB enables you to offload the administrative burden of operating and scaling a highly available database. It also provides consistent performance and availability at any scale.

DynamoDB Advantages

Performance

One of the main advantages of DynamoDB over traditional relational databases is its performance. DynamoDB is designed to offer fast and predictable performance at any scale. It uses a unique partitioning scheme that allows it to distribute data across multiple servers while maintaining low latency and high throughput.

Capacity

DynamoDB uses adaptive capacity to automatically adjust capacity in response to changing traffic patterns, so you never have to worry about over provisioning or under provisioning your database.

Scalability

DynamoDB scales horizontally by adding more servers as needed to handle increased traffic. It also scales vertically by adding more capacity to individual servers. This allows DynamoDB to handle sudden spikes in traffic without sacrificing performance or availability.

Availability

DynamoDB is also a highly available and fault tolerant database service. It uses multiple Availability Zones (AZs) to replicate the data across multiple servers in different geographic regions. This ensures that your data is always available, even if one or more AZs goes down.

Fault tolerance

DynamoDB automatically fails over to a standby replica in another AZ if the primary replica becomes unavailable.

Fully Managed

DynamoDB is a fully managed service, which means that Amazon takes care of all the administrative tasks associated with running a NoSQL database. This includes patching, backing up, monitoring, and scaling the database. This frees up your time so you can focus on developing your applications instead of worrying about managing the infrastructure.

Security and compliance

DynamoDB has encryption at rest and in transit: DynamoDB encrypts your data at rest using server side encryption with an AWS Key Management Service (KMS) master key. DynamoDB also supports client side encryption, which lets you encrypt your data before you send it to DynamoDB.

Auditing

DynamoDB can streams all data changes made to your table, which lets you track activity and monitor for suspicious activity. You can also use AWS CloudTrail to log all DynamoDB API calls for further auditing.

DynamoDB Disadvantages

While DynamoDB has many benefits, such as being highly scalable and offering low latency, there are also some disadvantages to using this service.

Expensive at Scale

DynamoDB can be more expensive than other database solutions, especially when you need to scale. The reason for this is that DynamoDB is a managed service, which means that AWS takes care of all the underlying infrastructure and management for you. This is great if you're not familiar with AWS or if you don't want to worry about managing your own database, but it comes at a cost. When you need to scale DynamoDB, you're essentially paying for AWS to do all the work for you, which can add up quickly.

NoSQL Database

DynamoDB is not a good choice for workloads that require a relational database structure. This is because DynamoDB is a NoSQL database, which means that it does not support the relational database model. In addition, DynamoDB does not support foreign keys, which are necessary for defining relationships between tables in a relational database. As a result, DynamoDB is not suitable for workloads that require a complex relational database structure.

Data Consistency

DynamoDB is not ACID compliant and does not support transactions that span multiple items or tables. This makes it an unsuitable choice for workloads that require a high level of data consistency, such as financial transactions.

Complex Queries

DynamoDB's lack of query optimization can make it difficult to execute complex queries efficiently. For example, a query that requires a full table scan can be very slow. Additionally, DynamoDB does not support indexing, so it can be difficult to find specific items in a large table.

In conclusion, DynamoDB is a great tool for those who need a powerful database solution. However, it does have its drawbacks. If you're looking for a lighthearted and witty take on the pros and cons of DynamoDB, this is the article for you!

item.title: 更好的编程生产力-技术和态度 | Better Programming Productivity- Techniques and Attitude
item.description:
作为一名程序员，你可以做很多事情来提高你的工作效率。 其中一些本质上是技术性的，例如使用支持代码完成和重构等功能的优秀文本编辑器或 IDE。 其他的更多是关于态度和方法，例如保持一致的编码风格，清晰地注释代码，以及对学习新事物和犯错有正确的态度。
要提高工作效率，您可以做的最重要的事情之一就是使用好的文本编辑器或 IDE。 一个好的编辑器将具有代码完成、重构、git 支持和更多插件等功能，所有这些都可以为您节省大量时间。 使用一致的编码风格也很重要，这样您的代码才易于阅读和理解。 当您需要稍后返回您的代码或与其他人一起工作时，这将为您提供帮助。

我可以证明有几次我在远程实例上工作并且忘记提交一些东西，期望它保存然后由于某种原因我所有的新代码都消失了，幸运的是 Pycharm 有它自己的版本控制来拯救我！，这个简单的功能被保存了我重做了一整天的工作。

另一个重要的事情是使用版本控制系统 (VCS)。 这将允许您跟踪对代码的更改并在必要时恢复。 它还使与其他人在项目上协作变得更容易。即使在我从事个人项目时，我也会努力编写好的提交，既要养成良好的习惯，又要保持一切整洁。 另一个很好的技术提示，它是 Activeley 寻找让您的生活更轻松的插件 - 每个开发人员都有自己的用例和偏好，所以不要害怕探索各种插件。

最后，对编程有正确的态度很重要。 这包括愿意学习新事物并且不怕犯错误。 错误是学习过程的一部分，从长远来看，它们可以帮助您成为更好的程序员。

你能改变你的心态吗？

是的你可以！ 这可能需要一些努力，而且不会一蹴而就，但如果你愿意投入工作，你绝对可以改变你的心态，变得更好。 以下是一些帮助您入门的提示：

改变心态的最好方法之一是专注于在你的思维中更加积极和乐观。 与其纠缠于消极的方面或可能出错的地方，不如试着想想什么是对的。 当你发现自己在想消极的想法时，停下来，把它们重新定义为更积极的东西。 例如，不要想“我永远做不到”，而是想“我能做到”。 这似乎是一个很小的变化，但它可以对你的感受和你采取行动的动力产生很大的影响。

改变心态的另一种方法是专注于你的目标和你想要实现的目标。 当你清楚自己想要什么时，保持动力并采取行动实现它会容易得多。 因此，与其关注所有你不想要的东西（比如超重或负债），不如关注你想要的东西（比如健康和财务自由）。 可以这么说，当您关注奖品时，保持专注并采取行动实现目标会容易得多。

生产力的最大杀手之一是拖延。 如果你总是推迟采取行动，那么实现任何实质性的事情都将非常困难。 因此，如果您想改变思维方式并提高工作效率，重要的是您要采取行动，不要让拖延成为阻碍。 当然，这说起来容易做起来难！ 如果你正在与拖延作斗争，你可以做一些事情来克服它：
设定较小的目标，而不是试图一次完成太多。 当一个目标感觉压倒一切时，完全推迟它通常更容易。 但是，如果您将其分解为更易于管理的较小步骤，则采取行动并取得进展会容易得多。

为自己创建一个时间表或截止日期。 有一个你需要完成某事的特定日期可能是一个很好的动力。 请确保不要让时间表过于不切实际，否则会适得其反！

寻找责任合作伙伴或团体。 让某人（或一群人）让您负责为实现您的目标而采取行动可能会有所帮助。 这可能是朋友、家人、同事，甚至是在线责任小组。 知道有人指望你可能只是你前进的动力！

改变你的心态不是一件容易的事，但通过一些努力和毅力绝对是可能的。 如果您想提高工作效率和成功率，首先要专注于在您的思维中更加积极和乐观，设定明确的目标，并为实现这些目标采取行动。 随着时间的推移和练习，你会很好地改变你的心态，变得更好！

总之，如果你想成为一个更有生产力的程序员，你需要有正确的技术和态度。 使用正确的工具和思维方式，您可以取得伟大的成就。

在评论中告诉我您有哪些提高生产力的方法 - 分享对您有帮助的工具、插件和思维方式！

原文:

As a programmer, there are many things you can do to improve your productivity. Some of these are technical in nature, such as using a good text editor or IDE that supports features like code completion and refactoring. Others are more about attitude and approach, such as maintaining a consistent coding style, commenting code clearly, and having the right attitude towards learning new things and making mistakes.
One of the most important things you can do to be productive is to use a good text editor or IDE. A good editor will have features like code completion, refactoring, git support and many more plugins, all of these can save you a lot of time. It’s also important to use a consistent coding style so that your code is easy to read and understand. This will help you when you need to come back to your code later or when working with other people.

I can attest to a few times I worked on remote instances and forgot to commit something, expecting it to save then for some reason all of my new code was gone, luckily Pycharm has it's own version control to save me!, that simple feature saved me redoing a whole days work.

Another important thing is to use a version control system (VCS). This will allow you to track changes to your code and revert back if necessary. It also makes it easier to collaborate with other people on projects.Even when I am working on a personal project I make an effort to write good commits, both to enforce good habits and to keep everything tidy. Another good technical tip it to activeley look for plugins that make your life easier - every developer has his own use cases and prefrences so don't be afraid to explore all kinds of plugins.

Finally, it’s important to have the right attitude towards programming. This includes being willing to learn new things and not being afraid to make mistakes. Mistakes are part of the learning process and they can help you become a better programmer in the long run.

Can you change your mindset?

Yes, you can! It may take some effort and it won't happen overnight, but if you're willing to put in the work, you can definitely change your mindset for the better. Here are a few tips to get you started:

1. Be more positive and optimistic in your thinking

One of the best ways to change your mindset is to focus on being more positive and optimistic in your thinking. Instead of dwelling on the negative or on what could go wrong, try to think about what could go right. When you catch yourself thinking negative thoughts, stop and reframe them into something more positive. For example, instead of thinking "I'll never be able to do this," try thinking "I can do this." It may seem like a small change, but it can make a big difference in how you feel and how motivated you are to take action.

2. Focus on your goals and what you want to achieve

Another way to change your mindset is to focus on your goals and what you want to achieve. When you're clear about what you want, it's much easier to stay motivated and take action towards achieving it. So instead of focusing on all the things you don't want (like being overweight or in debt), focus on what you do want (like being healthy and financially free). When you keep your eye on the prize, so to speak, it's much easier to stay focused and take action towards achieving your goals.

3. Take action and don't procrastinate

One of the biggest killers of productivity is procrastination. If you're constantly putting off taking action, it's going to be very difficult to achieve anything substantial. So if you want to change your mindset and be more productive, it's important that you take action and don't let procrastination get in the way. Of course, this is easier said than done! If you're struggling with procrastination, there are a few things you can do to overcome it:
Set smaller goals rather than trying to accomplish too much at once. When a goal feels overwhelming, it's often easier to just put it off altogether. But if you break it down into smaller steps that feel more manageable, it will be much easier to take action and make progress.

Create a timeline or deadline for yourself. Having a specific date that you need to have something accomplished by can be a great motivator. Just be sure not to make the timeline too unrealistic or else it will backfire!

Find an accountability partner or group. It can be helpful to have someone (or a group of people) who will hold you accountable for taking action towards your goals. This could be a friend, family member, coworker, or even an online accountability group. Knowing that someone is counting on you can be just the motivation you need to get moving!

Changing your mindset is not an easy task but it is definitely possible with some effort and perseverance. If you want to be more productive and successful, start by focusing on being more positive and optimistic in your thinking, setting clear goals, and taking action towards those goals. With time and practice,you'll be well on your wayto changingyour mindset forthe better!

In conclusion, if you want to be a more productive programmer, you need to have the right techniques and attitude. With the right tools and mindset, you can achieve great things.

Tell me in the comments what are your ways to improve productivity - share tools, plugins and mindsets that help you!

item.title: 社区需要支持 | Communities Need Support
item.description:
我花了很多时间与不同类型的社区打交道，与社区建设者接触，更多地了解社区建设，并有机地发展了几个社区。 我从这些经历中得到的收获之一是，如果只有一个人支持，社区就不会扩大规模。 那么社区需要发展什么？

买入

除非有买入，否则您无法扩展。 你需要有一个多对多的关系。 那不应该只是来自您的团队或公司的人。 但是，当您的社区正在建立时，您需要公司/团队的人员作为支持结构。 把事情搁置到解决为止。 请记住，您的团队将成为社区的榜样。 伟大的领导者创造伟大的领导者。

响应性

你需要反应灵敏。 几乎没有什么比进入一个安静的松弛或不和谐更糟糕的了。 这几乎就像进入一个大型自助餐厅，每个人都在默默地吃饭。 他们在那里，你可以看到他们，但没有人在说话。 我不了解你，但我会转身走另一条路。 您不需要立即做出回应，您应该让您的社区有机会相互回应，但未回答的问题是社区不健康的标志。

时间

社区是一场漫长的比赛。 你不会在一夜之间发展出一个繁荣的社区。 这需要时间，而且通常没有明确的指标来跟踪你在做什么。 社区需要真正地成长。 除非你已经拥有一个投资社区——例如 Jamstack 那样——那么你必须从基础开始，一砖一瓦地构建它，了解你的社区想要什么、他们是谁、他们想成为什么、他们想要什么需要。

请注意，如果您没有支持，您可能面临的挑战：您不仅必须承担更多的社区重量，而且您将不断努力证明自己并乞求人们的支持不了解或不希望将社区互动添加到他们的板块中的人。 这可能是你成功的最大障碍。 从支持开始是发展社区的最佳方式。 这不是唯一的方法，你当然可以不这样做，但发展社区和维持社区健康的挑战会更大。

一旦暂停，bekahhw 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，bekahhw 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，bekahhw 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 bekahhw 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 bekahhw 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 bekahhw 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I've been spending a lot of time engaging with different types of communities, taking to community builders, learning more about community building, and organically growing a couple of communities. And one of the takeaways I have from these experiences is that communities don’t scale if they only has one person supporting it. So what do communities need to grow?

Buy-in

You can’t scale unless there’s buy-in. You need to have a many-to-many relationship. That shouldn’t be people just from your team or company. But while your community is being built, you’ll need people from your company/team to be the support structure. To hold things up until they’re settled. And remember, your team will be the example for the community. Great leaders create great leaders.

Responsivity

You need to be responsive. There’s almost nothing worse than entering a quiet slack or discord. It’s almost like entering a large cafeteria and everyone is eating in silence. They’re there, you can see them, but no one is talking. I don't know about you, but I'd turn around and walk the other way. You don’t need to immediately respond, and you should give your community the opportunity to respond to each other, but unanswered questions are a sign of an unhealthy community.

Time

Community is a long game. You don’t grow a thriving community overnight. It takes time and there often aren’t clear metrics to track what you’re doing. Community needs to grow authentically. Unless you already have an invested community--like Jamstack did, for example--then you have to build it from the base, brick by brick, learning about what your community wants, who they are, where they want to be, what they need.

Just a note on the challenges you might face if you don’t have buy-in: Not only will you have to carry more of the weight of the community, but you’ll be constantly trying to prove yourself and beg for support from people who don’t understand or want to have community interaction added to their plates. This may be the biggest impediment to your success. Starting with support is the best way to grow a community. It's not the only way, and you can certainly start without that, but the challenge to growing a community and maintaining community health will be greater.

Emmanuel Fordjour Kumah - Mar 18

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag bekahhw:

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging bekahhw will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 一个只有 HTML 和 CSS 的多重配色方案选择器 | An HTML and CSS only multiple color scheme picker
item.description:
现在是 2022 年，许多网站都支持暗模式。 大约 7% 的网站在 2021 年做到了

这些网站中的许多甚至都有在明暗模式之间切换的开关。

黑暗模式很棒，很多人都喜欢它，它对眼睛更舒服，并且可以延长电池寿命。

但是其他配色方案呢？

对于我的网站missingdice.com ，我想支持两种以上的配色方案。

这是一个提供玩游戏工具的网站——掷骰子、纺车之类的东西——它很简单，而且很有趣。

该网站满足了非常简单的需求，因此相同的工具可能用于儿童派对，或用于玩龙与地下城的团体，或者可能是一些低赌注。

所以我决定加入light 、 dark 、 party 、 vegas和dungeons and dragons配色方案，以及high contrast mode ——这对于一些有视觉障碍、太阳镜或屏幕处于阳光直射下的人来说很有用。

我之前分享了一个关于如何制作仅html和css的暗模式切换的教程——一些人发现它很有用，并要求我发布一个关于颜色方案选择器的教程。 所以就在这里。

它涵盖了与暗模式切换大部分相同的领域。 它在没有JavaScript的情况下也能正常工作，但也为启用JavaScript的用户提供了一些渐进式增强功能。

这就是我们要做的……

一个配色方案选择器...

  • 没有javascript也能很好地工作
  • 默认为用户的dark mdoe偏好
  • 支持windows high-contrast模式和forced-colors

保存用户偏好

  • 使用正确的配色方案更新<meta rel="theme"/>和<body>标记。

    • 如果不支持 css 变量（自定义属性），则回退到light mode

  • 如果不支持 css 变量，请删除切换。

    • 使用什么样的输入

    由于我们有多个值，并且一次只能选择一个——我们只有两个选择。 由于我们的no-js限制——我们需要使用可以在 CSS 中访问状态的输入。

    选择框<option>元素嵌套在<select>元素中——这使得使用 CSS 的页面的其余部分无法访问它们的状态。

    所以我们留下了radio buttons ——无论如何这是一个不错的选择！

    这个怎么运作

    要仅使用 css 更改页面的配色方案 - 我们需要确保我们的单选按钮出现在文档中的所有其他内容之前。

    我们的每种配色方案都有一个无线电输入。 然后我们可以使用:checked css 选择器来定位文档的其余部分。

    如果选择了id=blue的单选输入，我们使用:checked css 选择器来定位文档的其余部分。 我们不能根据单选输入设置<body>标签的样式。 因此，我们创建了一个<div> ，我们的网站的内容进入其中，然后为该<div>设置样式以填充屏幕。

    当overscrolling和在非矩形屏幕上时，这会导致一个小问题，但我们稍后会通过一些渐进增强来解决这个问题。

    然后我们可以在视觉上隐藏<input>元素，并使用它们对应的标签作为按钮来切换配色方案。

    标签可以放置在页面上的任何位置，只要标签的for属性与其对应的无线电输入的id匹配，就可以正常工作。

     <body> <input id= "dark" class= "color-scheme-button" name= "color-scheme" type= "radio" /> <input id= "light" class= "color-scheme-button" name= "color-scheme" type= "radio" /> <input id= "blue" class= "color-scheme-button" name= "color-scheme" type= "radio" /> <!-- more radio buttons go here --> <div class= "color-scheme-wrapper" > <label for= "dark" > dark mode </label> <label for= "light" > light mode </label> <label for= "blue" > blue mode </label> <!-- Site Content --> </div> <style> .color-scheme-wrapper { min-height : 100vh ; background : white ; color : black ; } .color-scheme-wrapper { background : white ; color : black ; } #dark :checked ~ * { background : black ; color : white ; } #blue :checked ~ * { background : blue ; color : white ; } </style> </body>

    此代码有效。 但是我们仍然需要添加更多的东西。 我们将从 CSS 变量开始。

    相反，我们可以定义一些变量，然后根据所选的配色方案更改它们的值。

    为了本教程的目的，我们将保持简单——一种颜色用于背景，一种颜色用于文本。

    首先，我们为默认配色方案创建值——这将是浅色主题：

     :root { --background : white ; --text : black ; }

    然后我们对其他配色方案做同样的事情——这次将变量应用于匹配该方案的选中单选按钮之后的每个元素。

    如果用户选中一个单选按钮，则该配色方案的变量将应用于整个站点。

     #dark :checked ~ * { --bg : black ; --text : white ; } #blue :checked ~ * { --bg : blue ; --text : white ; } .color-scheme-wrapper { background : var ( --bg ); color : var ( --text ); }

    这是可行的——但我们需要对不支持 CSS 变量的浏览器进行后备——在撰写本文时，这对~3.5%的网络用户来说是正确的。

    因此，我们可以通过重复该属性两次来使我们的网站在旧浏览器上看起来不错，一次使用后备颜色，然后再次使用我们的变量，如下所示：

     .color-scheme-wrapper { background : white ; background : var ( --bg ); color : black ; color : var ( --text ); }

    我们还可以为浏览器不支持的用户隐藏配色方案按钮：

     .color-scheme-button , .color-scheme-button + label { display : none ; } @supports ( --css : custom-properties ) { .color-scheme-button , .color-scheme-button + label { display : block ; } }

    默认为用户的首选配色方案

    我们当前的设置不支持首选配色方案 - 这意味着如果在其设备上启用了深色模式的用户访问我们的网站，他们将看到我们的浅色主题。

    使用prefers-color-scheme css 媒体查询——我们可以确保访问者的首选配色方案是默认的。

    如果用户的设备启用了dark-mode ，我们通过“交换”浅色主题和深色主题的变量来做到这一点——使默认配色方案“深色”和dark主题“浅色”。

     :root { --bg : white ; --text : black ; } @media ( prefers-color-scheme : dark ) { :root { --bg : black ; --text : white ; } } #dark :checked ~ * { --bg : black ; --text : white ; } @media ( prefers-color-scheme : dark ) { #dark :checked ~ * { --bg : white ; --text : black ; } }

    太好了，现在如果用户启用了暗模式，网站将默认为暗模式 - 但是，如果用户想要手动选择light mode ，他们需要选择带有“暗模式”标签的单选按钮。

    一个小障碍，但它有一个简单的修复。

    我们可以使用相同的媒体查询来更改<label>元素中的文本。

    我们创建两个<span>元素，标签中包含文本“Light mode”和“Dark mode”——然后创建类以根据用户的偏好显示相关文本。

     <body> <div class= "color-scheme-wrapper" > <label for= "light" > <span class= "dark-mode-hide" > Light Mode </span> <span class= "light-mode-hide" > Dark Mode </span> </label> <label for= "dark" > <span class= "dark-mode-hide" > Dark Mode </span> <span class= "light-mode-hide" > Light Mode </span> </label> </div> </body> <style> .light-mode-hide { display : none } @media ( prefers-color-scheme : dark ) { .light-mode-hide { display : inline } .dark-mode-hide { display : none } } </style>

    现在我们的标签更改名称以匹配。

    强制颜色和高对比度模式

    由于各种原因，一些用户更喜欢将特定的配色方案应用于他们访问的每个网页。

    对于这些用户，在我们的网站上选择配色方案将无效。

    使用 css mediwe 可以删除启用了以下任一选项的用户的切换： forced colors或windows high contrast mode 。

    我们还可以使用这些 css 媒体查询对我们的设计进行其他细微的更改。

    在 Windows 8 上的 Internet Explorer 11 中引入，并继续在 Edge 上受支持。

     @media ( -ms-high-contrast : active ) { .color-scheme-button , .color-scheme-button + label { display : none ; } }

    强制颜色

    强制颜色允许用户为网络选择自己的配色方案。 它通常用于创建高对比度的配色方案，但也可用于为其他目的制作配色方案。

     @media ( forced-colors : active ) { .color-scheme-button , .color-scheme-button + label { display : none ; } }

    渐进式增强

    主题颜色元标记

    在此处查看实际使用的主题颜色示例

    它看起来像这样：

     <meta name= "theme-color" content= "white" />

    您还可以使用带有这些元标记的媒体查询，并让它们与用户的首选配色方案相匹配：

     <meta name= "theme-color" media= "(prefers-color-scheme: light)" content= "white" > <meta name= "theme-color" media= "(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" content= "black" >

    样式化body标签

    在某些浏览器中，当用户滚动或屏幕有大圆角时， <body>标签的颜色会可见——比如新的 iphone 和像素手机。 所以我们还应该使用我们的默认配色方案来设置 body 标签的样式：

     body { background : white ; } @media ( prefers-color-scheme : dark ) { background : black ; }

    这些非常适合设置默认值——但是当我们的用户更改配色方案时， theme-color元标记和正文颜色将保持不变。

    要解决这个问题需要 JavaScript——所以我们需要编写一些代码来更新元标记。

    首先，我们将创建一个具有与单选按钮的id匹配的键的对象——这些值是我们想要用来设置主题颜色的颜色。

     var themeColors = { dark : " black " , light : " white " , blue : " blue " }

    我们需要访问 javascript 中的元标记和正文。 为了使我们的代码简单，我给出了元标记 ID：

     <meta id= "light-theme-meta-tag" name= "theme-color" media= "(prefers-color-scheme: light)" content= "white" > <meta id= "dark-theme-meta-tag" name= "theme-color" media= "(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" content= "black" >

    然后我们像这样访问它们：

     var lightThemeMetaTag = document . getElementById ( ' light-theme-meta-tag ' ) var darkThemeMetaTag = document . getElementById ( ' dark-theme-meta-tag ' ) var body = document . body

    然后我们将编写一个函数来更新我们的元标记和我们的身体颜色。 它将用户选择的主题作为参数。

    我们还将添加一个变量selectedTheme来存储当前选择的主题，以便其他功能可以访问它——我们很快就会看到原因

    var selectedTheme = ' light ' function updateTheme ( theme ) { var color = themeColors [ theme ] lightThemeMetaTag . setAttribute ( " content " , color ); darkThemeMetaTag . setAttribute ( " content " , color ); body . style . backgroundColor = color ; selectedTheme = theme }

    然后我们将监听收音机输入的变化。

    如果检测到更改，我们将使用所选无线电输入的id调用updateTheme函数。

     var radios = document . querySelectorAll ( ' input[type=radio][name="color-scheme"] ' ); radios . forEach ( radio => radio . addEventListener ( ' change ' , ( event ) => { var theme = event . target . id updateTheme ( theme ) }));

    这会更新我们的元标记以匹配当前选择的主题。

    不过有一个小问题——在黑暗模式下，我们的明暗主题的值是错误的！

    因此，如果启用了暗模式，我们将添加一些代码来交换颜色。 它还将侦听设备的首选配色方案并进行相应更新。 例如，有些人将他们的设备设置为在特定时间切换到暗模式。

    首先，我们将编写一个函数，在我们的themeColors对象中交换浅色和深色。

     function swapLightAndDark (){ var light = themeColors . light var dark = themeColors . dark themeColors . dark = light themeColors . light = dark }

    然后我们添加代码，如果设备处于暗模式，则交换主题 - 如果用户的设备更改其首选配色方案，则再次交换它们。

    对prefers-color-scheme变量的更改也需要调用我们的setTheme函数来触发更改。 这是selectedTheme变量派上用场的地方。

     var preferedColorScheme = window . matchMedia ( ' (prefers-color-scheme: dark) ' ); var dark = preferedColorScheme . matches if ( dark ){ swapLightAndDark () } preferedColorScheme . addEventListener ( ' change ' , () => { swapLightAndDark () setTheme ( selectedTheme ) });

    记住用户偏好

    不使用 JavaScript 的最大问题是我们的用户偏好不会在访问之间被记住——甚至在我们网站上的页面之间。

    因此，对于启用 JavaScript 的用户，我们将使用local storage来存储他们的偏好。

    首先，我们将编写一个将配色方案保存到本地存储的函数。 它将当前主题作为参数：

     function saveThemeToLocalStorage ( theme ){ if ( localStorage ){ localStorage . setItem ( ' color-scheme ' , theme ); } }

    然后另一个函数检查用户是否在本地存储中保存了配色方案——如果有，我们将更新主题并确保我们将该主题的单选输入设置为checked 。

     function getThemeFromLocalStorage (){ if ( localStorage ){ var savedColorScheme = localStorage . getItem ( ' color-scheme ' ); if ( savedColorScheme ){ var radioButton = document . getElementById ( savedColorScheme ); radioButton . checked = true ; updateTheme ( savedColorScheme ) } } }

    把它们放在一起

    然后我们可以在我们之前编写的代码中使用这些功能。 把它们放在一起看起来像这样：

     var themeColors = { dark : " black " , light : " white " , blue : " blue " } var lightThemeMetaTag = document . getElementById ( ' light-theme-meta-tag ' ) var darkThemeMetaTag = document . getElementById ( ' dark-theme-meta-tag ' ) var body = document . body var selectedTheme = ' light ' function updateTheme ( theme ) { var color = themeColors [ theme ] lightThemeMetaTag . setAttribute ( " content " , color ); darkThemeMetaTag . setAttribute ( " content " , color ); body . style . backgroundColor = color ; selectedTheme = theme saveThemeToLocalStorage ( theme ) } function swapLightAndDark (){ var light = themeColors . light var dark = themeColors . dark themeColors . dark = light themeColors . light = dark } function saveThemeToLocalStorage ( theme ){ if ( localStorage ){ localStorage . setItem ( ' color-scheme ' , theme ); } } function getThemeFromLocalStorage (){ if ( localStorage ){ var savedColorScheme = localStorage . getItem ( ' color-scheme ' ); if ( savedColorScheme ){ var radioButton = document . getElementById ( savedColorScheme ); radioButton . checked = true ; updateTheme ( savedColorScheme ) } } } var preferedColorScheme = window . matchMedia ( ' (prefers-color-scheme: dark) ' ); var dark = preferedColorScheme . matches if ( dark ){ swapLightAndDark () } preferedColorScheme . addEventListener ( ' change ' , () => { swapLightAndDark () setTheme ( selectedTheme ) }); var radios = document . querySelectorAll ( ' input[type=radio][name="color-scheme"] ' ); radios . forEach ( radio => radio . addEventListener ( ' change ' , ( event ) => { var theme = event . target . id updateTheme ( theme ) })); // This must be called after the check for dark mode getThemeFromLocalStorage ()

    最后，为了确保在页面呈现之前从本地存储中加载我们的主题，我们需要将此 JavaScript 放置在无线电输入之后但页面其余部分之前的标记中。

    理想情况下，这是内联的，以阻止页面呈现被 http 请求延迟。

     <body> <input id= "dark" class= "color-scheme-button" name= "color-scheme" type= "radio" /> <input id= "light" class= "color-scheme-button" name= "color-scheme" type= "radio" /> <input id= "blue" class= "color-scheme-button" name= "color-scheme" type= "radio" /> <script> <!-- our color scheme script goes here --> </script> <div class= "color-scheme-wrapper" > <!-- Site Content --> </div>

    而已。

    而已！。 使用上面的代码和一些样式，您可以制作一个非常有用的配色方案选择器，它适用于尽可能多的人。

    我在 codepen 中创建了一个演示。 它使用了上面的所有代码，但是有一些额外的样式，以及一个用于选择配色方案的下拉菜单，这里是：

    最后的一些想法

    为配色方案使用下拉菜单

    我探索了多种显示配色方案选项的方法。 但最后我选择为每个选项使用带有标准外观单选按钮的下拉菜单。

    这使我可以在不占用整个屏幕的情况下显示每种配色方案的完整标签——以及一个解释按钮功能的清晰标签。

    我尝试过

    • 在每个选项旁边显示配色方案的预览
    • 使用每个主题的颜色为不带文本的按钮设置样式
    • 创建一个多拨动开关外观的东西
    • 有一个按钮可以循环浏览每个颜色主题。

    我把这些给朋友看了，他们大多没有意识到我们改变颜色主题的目的。 显示配色方案的预览也很难破译，尤其是在主题与当前主题相似的情况下。

    因此，带有清晰文本标签的下拉菜单是赢家。

    不使用 JS 保存配色方案！？

    这个配色方案选择器最初是为missingdice.com创建的。 当我不想做任何高风险的事情时，这是我从事的一个项目。 它有很多关于它应该如何工作的自我强加的规则。

    其中一条规则是每个工具都应该在没有JavaScript的情况下工作。

    例如，如果用户禁用了 JavaScript 并且他们使用掷骰子工具。 它不是在客户端掷骰子，而是向服务器提交一个表单，服务器掷骰子，然后发回一个带有结果的页面。

    这很好用！ 但是，这意味着我们可爱的 no-js 颜色切换器实际上变得无用了。 用户选择一种颜色，然后提交表单，他们的颜色偏好消失了，他们的滚动结果以默认模式显示。

    那么，如何解决这个问题？

    我们将整个页面放在一个<form>中，并提交我们的用户选择的配色方案以及他们的掷骰子选项！ 然后我们确保服务器以他们首选的配色方案显示的结果进行响应。

    我很快就会把它添加到网站上，敬请期待。



    原文:

    It's 2022 and lots of websites support dark mode. Around 7% of websites did in 2021

    Many of these websites even have a toggle for changing between light and dark mode.

    Dark mode is great, many people prefer it, it's easier on the eyes, and it extends battery life.

    But what about other color schemes?

    For my website missingdice.com I wanted to support more than two color schemes.

    It's a website with tools for playing games — rolling dice, spinning wheels, that sort of thing — it's simple and it's for fun.

    The site fulfils quite simple needs, so the same tool might be used for a kid's party, or by a group playing dungeons and dragons, or possibly some low stakes betting.

    So i decided to include light, dark, party, vegas, and dungeons and dragons color schemes, and a high contrast mode — useful for some people with visual impairments, sunglasses, or if the screen is in direct sunlight.

    I previously shared a tutorial on how to make a html and css only dark mode toggle — some found it useful and requested I publish a tutorial for the color-scheme picker. So here it is.

    It covers much of the same ground as the dark mode toggle. It works fine without JavaScript, but also has some progressive enhancements for users with JavaScript enabled.

    Here's what we're going to make…

    A color scheme picker that…

    • works well without javascript
    • defaults to a user's dark mdoe preference
    • supports windows high-contrast mode and forced-colors

    …as well as some progressive enhancements for users with JavaScript enabled…

    • Saves user preferences
    • Updates the <meta rel="theme"/> and <body> tag with the correct color scheme.

    …and some graceful degradations for users on older browsers…

    • falls back to light mode if css variables (custom properties) aren't supported
    • remove the toggle if css variables aren't supported.

    What kind of input to use

    Since we have multiple values, and can only select one at a time — we have only two choices. Radio Buttons or a Select Box.

    Due to our no-js limitation — we need to use an input who's state can be accessed in CSS.

    Select box <option> elements are nested inside a <select> element — this makes their state inaccessible to the rest of the page using CSS.

    So we're left with radio buttons — which is a good choice anyway!

    How it works

    To change our page's color scheme using just css — we need to make sure our radio buttons appear before everything else in our document.

    We have a radio input for each of our color schemes. Then we can target the rest of our document with the :checked css selector.

    if the radio input with id=blue is selected, we use the :checked css selector to target the rest of our document. The css selector looks like this: #blue:checked ~ *.

    We can't style the <body> tag based on the radio inputs. So we create a <div> that our site's content goes inside, then style that <div> to fill the screen.

    This causes a small issue when overscrolling and on non-rectangular screens, but we'll solve that later with some progressive enhancements.

    We can then visually hide the <input> elements, and use their corresponding labels as buttons to toggle color scheme.

    The labels can be placed anywhere on the page and will work fine so long as the label's for attribute matches it's corresponding radio input's id.

    <body> <input id="dark" class="color-scheme-button" name="color-scheme" type="radio"/>
<input id="light" class="color-scheme-button" name="color-scheme" type="radio"/>
<input id="blue" class="color-scheme-button" name="color-scheme" type="radio"/> <!-- more radio buttons go here --> <div class="color-scheme-wrapper"> <label for="dark">dark mode</label> <label for="light">light mode</label> <label for="blue">blue mode</label> <!-- Site Content --> </div> <style> .color-scheme-wrapper { min-height:100vh; background:white; color:black;
} .color-scheme-wrapper { background:white; color:black;
} #dark:checked ~ * { background:black; color:white;
} #blue:checked ~ * { background:blue; color:white;
} </style> </body>

    This code works. But we still need to add a few more things. We'll start with CSS variables.

    CSS variables (custom properties)

    css variables save us a lot of clutter — without them we'd have to restyle every component on our website for each color scheme.

    Instead we can define some variables and then change their values based on the selected color scheme.

    For the sake of this tutorial we'll keep things simple — one color for the background and one for text.

    First we create values for the default color scheme — this will be the light theme:

    
:root { --background:white; --text:black; }

    Then we do the same for the other color schemes — this time applying the variables to every element that comes after the checked radio button that matches that scheme.

    If a user checks a radio button, that color schemes' variables are applied to the whole site.

    
#dark:checked ~ * { --bg:black; --text:white; } #blue:checked ~ * { --bg:blue; --text:white; } .color-scheme-wrapper { background:var(--bg); color:var(--text); }

    This works — but we need a fallback for browsers that don't support CSS variables — which as of writing is true for ~3.5% of web users.

    css is very forgiving — if a browser comes accross a property it doesn't understand it will ignore it and keep going.

    So, we can make our site look good on older browsers by repeating the property twice, once with a fallback color, and then again with our variable, like so:

    .color-scheme-wrapper { background:white; background:var(--bg); color:black; color:var(--text); }

    We can also hide our color scheme buttons for users who's browsers don't support them:

    .color-scheme-button,
.color-scheme-button + label { display:none; } @supports(--css:custom-properties) { .color-scheme-button, .color-scheme-button + label { display:block; }
}

    Defaulting to user's preferred color scheme

    Our current setup doesn't support preferred color scheme — meaning if a user who's has dark mode enabled on their device visits our site, they'll be met with our light theme.

    Using the prefers-color-scheme css media query — we can make sure our visitor's preferred color scheme is the default.

    We do this by "swapping" the variables of the light theme and dark theme if the user's device has dark-mode enabled — making the default color scheme "dark" and the dark theme "light".

    
:root { --bg:white; --text:black; } @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { :root { --bg:black; --text:white; }
} #dark:checked ~ * { --bg:black; --text:white; } @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { #dark:checked ~ * { --bg:white; --text:black; } }

    That's great, now if a user has dark mode enabled, the site will default to dark mode — however, if the user then wants to manually select light mode they need to select the radio button with the "Dark mode" label.

    A small snag, but it has a simple fix.

    We can use the same media queries to change the text in the <label> elements.

    We create two <span> elements with the text "Light mode" and "Dark mode" in the labels — then create classes to display the relevant text based on the user's preferences.

    
<body> <div class="color-scheme-wrapper"> <label for="light"> <span class="dark-mode-hide">Light Mode</span> <span class="light-mode-hide">Dark Mode</span> </label> <label for="dark"> <span class="dark-mode-hide">Dark Mode</span> <span class="light-mode-hide">Light Mode</span> </label> </div> </body> <style> .light-mode-hide { display:none
} @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { .light-mode-hide { display:inline } .dark-mode-hide { display:none }
}
</style>

    Now our label's change names to match.

    Forced Colors and High Contrast Mode

    For a variety of reasons, some users prefer specific color schemes to be applied to every web page they visit.

    For these users selecting a color scheme on our website will have no effect.

    Using css mediwe can remove the toggle for users with who have enabled either: forced colors or windows high contrast mode.

    We can also use these css media queries to make other subtle changes to our design.

    Windows High Contrast mode

    -ms-high-contrast non-standard (microsoft only) css media feature. Introduced on Internet Explorer 11 on Windows 8 and continues to be supported on Edge.

    @media (-ms-high-contrast: active) { .color-scheme-button,
.color-scheme-button + label { display:none; } }

    Forced colors

    Forced colors allows users to select their own color scheme for the web. It's often used to create high contrast color schemes, but can be used to make color schemes for other purposes too.

    
@media (forced-colors: active) { .color-scheme-button, .color-scheme-button + label { display:none; }
}

    Progressive Enhancements

    Theme color meta tag

    The theme-color meta tag attribute adds color to the browser's user interface (ui) — usually to the browser toolbar. See examples of theme-color in action here

    It looks like this:

    <meta name="theme-color" content="white" />

    You can also use media queries with these meta tags and have them match the user's preferred color scheme:

    <meta name="theme-color" media="(prefers-color-scheme: light)" content="white">
<meta name="theme-color" media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" content="black">

    Styling the body tag

    In some browsers the color of the <body> tag will be visible when the user overscrolls or if the screen has large rounded corners — like the new iphones and pixel phones. So we should also style the body tag with our default color schemes:

    body { background:white; } @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) { background:black; }

    These are great for setting defaults — but when our users change the color scheme the theme-color meta tags and the body color will stay the same.

    To fix this requires JavaScript — so we need to write some code to update the meta tags.

    Enhancements with JavaScript

    First we'll create an object with keys matching the ids of the radio buttons — the values are the colors we want to use to set the theme colors.

    
var themeColors = { dark: "black", light: "white", blue: "blue" }

    We need to access the meta tags and the body in our javascript. To keep our code simple I've given the meta tags ids:

    <meta id="light-theme-meta-tag" name="theme-color" media="(prefers-color-scheme: light)" content="white">
<meta id="dark-theme-meta-tag" name="theme-color" media="(prefers-color-scheme: dark)" content="black">

    Then we access them like so:

    var lightThemeMetaTag = document.getElementById('light-theme-meta-tag')
var darkThemeMetaTag = document.getElementById('dark-theme-meta-tag')
var body = document.body

    Then we'll write a function that updates our meta tags and our body color. It takes user selected theme as a parameter.

    We'll also add a variable selectedTheme to store the currently selected theme so it's accessible to other functions — we'll see why shortly

    
var selectedTheme = 'light' function updateTheme(theme) { var color = themeColors[theme] lightThemeMetaTag.setAttribute("content", color); darkThemeMetaTag.setAttribute("content", color); body.style.backgroundColor = color; selectedTheme = theme }

    Then we’ll listen for changes to our radio inputs.

    If a change is detected, we’ll call the updateTheme function with id of the selected radio input.

    
var radios = document.querySelectorAll('input[type=radio][name="color-scheme"]'); radios.forEach(radio => radio.addEventListener('change', (event) => { var theme = event.target.id updateTheme(theme) }));

    That updates our meta tags to match the currently selected theme.

    There's one small problem though — on dark mode the values for our light and dark theme are the wrong way round!

    So we’ll add some code that swaps the colors round if dark mode is enabled. It will also listens in the device's preferred color scheme and update accordingly. For instance, some people have their devices set to shift to dark mode at certain times.

    First we'll write a function that swaps the light and dark colors around in our themeColors object.

    
function swapLightAndDark(){ var light = themeColors.light var dark = themeColors.dark themeColors.dark = light themeColors.light = dark }

    Then we add code the swaps the themes round if the device is in dark mode — and swaps them again if the user's device changes it's prefered color scheme.

    Changes to the prefers-color-scheme variable also needs to call our setTheme function to trigger the changes. This is where the selectedTheme variable comes in handy.

    
var preferedColorScheme = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)');
var dark = preferedColorScheme.matches if(dark){ swapLightAndDark()
} preferedColorScheme.addEventListener('change', () => { swapLightAndDark() setTheme(selectedTheme) });

    Remembering user preferences

    The biggest issue with not using JavaScript is that our user's preferences won't be remembered between visits — or even between pages on our site.

    So, for users with JavaScript enabled we'll use local storage to store their preference.

    First we'll write a function that saves the color scheme to local storage. It takes the current theme as a parameter:

    function saveThemeToLocalStorage(theme){ if(localStorage){ localStorage.setItem('color-scheme', theme); } }

    Then another function that checks if the user has a saved color scheme in local storage — if they do we'll update the theme and make sure we set that theme's radio input to checked.

    
function getThemeFromLocalStorage(){ if(localStorage){ var savedColorScheme = localStorage.getItem('color-scheme'); if(savedColorScheme){ var radioButton = document.getElementById(savedColorScheme); radioButton.checked = true; updateTheme(savedColorScheme) } } }

    Putting it all together

    Then we can those function in our previously written code. Putting it all together looks like this:

    var themeColors = { dark: "black", light: "white", blue: "blue" } var lightThemeMetaTag = document.getElementById('light-theme-meta-tag')
var darkThemeMetaTag = document.getElementById('dark-theme-meta-tag')
var body = document.body var selectedTheme = 'light' function updateTheme(theme) { var color = themeColors[theme] lightThemeMetaTag.setAttribute("content", color); darkThemeMetaTag.setAttribute("content", color); body.style.backgroundColor = color; selectedTheme = theme saveThemeToLocalStorage(theme) } function swapLightAndDark(){ var light = themeColors.light var dark = themeColors.dark themeColors.dark = light themeColors.light = dark } function saveThemeToLocalStorage(theme){ if(localStorage){ localStorage.setItem('color-scheme', theme); } } function getThemeFromLocalStorage(){ if(localStorage){ var savedColorScheme = localStorage.getItem('color-scheme'); if(savedColorScheme){ var radioButton = document.getElementById(savedColorScheme); radioButton.checked = true; updateTheme(savedColorScheme) } } } var preferedColorScheme = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)');
var dark = preferedColorScheme.matches if(dark){ swapLightAndDark()
} preferedColorScheme.addEventListener('change', () => { swapLightAndDark() setTheme(selectedTheme)
}); var radios = document.querySelectorAll('input[type=radio][name="color-scheme"]'); radios.forEach(radio => radio.addEventListener('change', (event) => { var theme = event.target.id updateTheme(theme) })); // This must be called after the check for dark mode
getThemeFromLocalStorage()

    Finally, in order to make sure our theme is loaded from local storage before the page renders, we need to place this JavaScript in a tag after the radio inputs, but before the rest of our page.

    Ideally this is inlined, to stop the page rendering being delayed by an http requrest.

    
<body> <input id="dark" class="color-scheme-button" name="color-scheme" type="radio"/>
<input id="light" class="color-scheme-button" name="color-scheme" type="radio"/>
<input id="blue" class="color-scheme-button" name="color-scheme" type="radio"/> <script> <!-- our color scheme script goes here -->
</script> <div class="color-scheme-wrapper"> <!-- Site Content --> </div>

    That's it.

    That's it!. With the above code and some styling you can make a very usable color scheme picker that works for as many people as possible.

    I've created a demo in codepen. It uses all the code above, but with some extra styling, and a dropdown menu for selecting the color schemes, here it is:

    Some final thoughts

    Using a drop down for the color schemes

    I explored a number of ways of displaying the color scheme options. But in the end I chose to use a drop down with a standard looking radio button for each option.

    This allowed me to display the full labels for each color scheme without taking over the whole screen — as well as a clear label explaining what the buttons do.

    I experimented with

    • showing a preview of the color scheme next to each option
    • styling buttons without text with the color of each theme
    • creating a multi-toggle switch looking thing
    • having a single button that cycles through each color theme.

    I showed these to friends, and they mostly didn’t realize what they we’re for changing the color theme. Showing previes of the color scheme was also hard to decipher, especially if the theme was similar to the current theme.

    So, a drop down with clear text labels was the winner.

    Saving color schemes without JS!?

    This color scheme picker was first created for missingdice.com. It's a project I work on when I don't feel like doing anything high stakes. It has lots of self-imposed rules about how it should work.

    One of these rules is that every tool should work without JavaScript.

    For instance, if a user has JavaScript disabled and they use the dice rolling tool. Instead of rolling dice on the client side, it submits a form to a server, which rolls the dice, and sends back a page with the result.

    This works great! BUT, it means our lovely no-js color switcher effectively becomes useless. A user picks a color, then they submit the form, and their color preference is gone, the results of their roll are shown in the default mode.

    So, how to get round this?

    We place our entire page inside a <form>, and submit our users chosen color scheme along with the options for their dice roll! Then we make sure the server responds with results shown in their preferred color scheme.

    I'll be adding that to the site soon, so stay tuned.
item.author: shadowfaxrodeo

item.title: Docusaurus 2.0：Meta 的静态网站生成器，用于构建精美的文档网站 | Docusaurus 2.0: Meta's static site generator to build beautiful documentation websites
item.description:
大家好！

这是我维护的Meta Open Source (Facebook)项目：一个基于 Node.js 和 React 的文档静态站点生成器。 它使您可以专注于内容：只需编写 Markdown 文件。 它已经为许多你知道的优秀文档网站提供支持：React-Native、Supabase、Redux、Tauri、Electron、Courier、Ionic、Figma、Snapchat、Prettier、Babel、Solana、Cardano……

注意：请务必阅读官方宣布 Docusaurus 2.0的帖子：比这篇更详细，这是我的个人总结。

什么是 Docusaurus？

写文档已经够难的了，而且如果你每次都必须定制一个网站，那它就变成了一个时间槽。

它有点像 Create-React-App，但用于创建内容站点：文档、博客、登录页面......

走向 Docusaurus v2

随着 v1 的发布，Meta 意识到了 v1 的一些局限性：

  • 使用 React 但仅在服务器端：没有水合，也没有单页应用导航
  • 回购非常单一，难以维护和测试
  • 不是很可扩展，没有插件系统
  • 主题相对有限，v1 网站看起来非常相似

插件系统：允许社区贡献有用的功能（OpenAPI 集成、本地搜索引擎、Sass 支持......）

  • 更灵活的主题系统：可以覆盖主题的任何 React 组件
  • 我们推迟了这个版本，因为之前尊重语义版本控制并不明显。

    今天，Docusaurus v2 提供了很多功能，很难一一列举：文档、博客、版本控制、主题、暗模式、i18n、a11y、SEO、TypeScript、PWA、Algolia DocSearch、MDX...

    谁使用 Docusaurus 2.0？

    许多公司已经采用 Docusaurus 2.0，尽管它处于测试阶段。 我们的网站展示已经包含近 300 个网站。

    而且还远远不够详尽，因为还没有添加这么多网站： Figma 、 Snapchat 、 Courier 、 1Password ......

    更不用说微软、LinkedIn、Shopify 等公司的许多内部网站......

    一些例子：

    • 大科技：微软、Snapchat、Shopify、LinkedIn、Meta、SAP、字节跳动、Lacework、Palo Alto Network……
    • 初创公司：Supabase、Ionic、Hasura、Figma、Courier、CodeSandbox、StackBlitz、Redis、Algolia、Hashnode...
    • 我们还有很多网站，这些网站的定制非常漂亮，真正尊重公司的品牌形象。

      我在这一切中的角色？

      两年前，当我在 Twitter 上滚动时，我遇到了这个：

      专业提示：加入 Twitter 并保持开放 😄 它可以带来有趣的职业机会！

      我认为这是一个很好的机会：作为一个开源项目的自由职业者在 Facebook 工作并不是每天都会发生的。 它应该持续 3 个月，但最终持续了更长的时间：我已经在 Docusaurus 上工作了 2 年多！

      我有机会以一种非常灵活的方式工作，拥有很大的自主权，这也让我可以同时运行这份时事通讯。

      看到 Docusaurus 流行起来真的很酷，我真的觉得我的工作产生了真正的影响。 我希望我已经启发了您尝试使用此工具，我迫不及待地想看到您创建的网站！ 不要忘记将它们发送到我们的网站展示！

      帮帮我们

      如果您能帮助我们使发射成功，我将不胜感激。 点赞、转发或评论对我们有很大帮助！

      我们推出的主要平台：

      • 谢谢，很快就会看到一些新的 React 新闻 :)

        我们最可爱的吉祥物 Slash 💕

    • 克里斯蒂安·费尔南多—— 一旦暂停，sebastienlorber 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，sebastienlorber 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，sebastienlorber 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 sebastienlorber 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 sebastienlorber 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 sebastienlorber 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Hello everyone!

    🎉 It is the official launch of Docusaurus 2.0!

    It is the Meta Open Source (Facebook) project I maintain: a documentation static-site-generator, based on Node.js and React. It allows you to focus on content: just write Markdown files. Docusaurus is truly customizable and respects your company's branding. It is already powering many great docs sites you know: React-Native, Supabase, Redux, Tauri, Electron, Courier, Ionic, Figma, Snapchat, Prettier, Babel, Solana, Cardano...

    🙏 We are also launching ProductHunt, Hacker News and Twitter: if you like the project, please vote and share your opinion 😃

    🎙️ My podcast on Docusaurus with The Developer's Bakery just came out too 😉

    Note: make sure to read the official Announcing Docusaurus 2.0 post: it is more detailed than this one, which is my personal summary.

    What is Docusaurus?

    Meta has a lot of internal/external projects, which have to be documented each time. It's hard enough to write docs, and if you have to customize a site each time, it becomes a time sink.

    In 2017 they started copy/pasting a Jekyll template for each project, but it quickly became difficult to maintain.

    In 2017, they decided to create Docusaurus, a React-based static site generator in Node.js, which will "hide" the technical stack of the site and allow to focus on the content: writing Markdown files. It is a bit like Create-React-App, but for creating content sites: docs, blog, landing pages...

    The v1 of Docusaurus quickly became popular, used by React-Native, Jest, Babel, Prettier...

    Toward Docusaurus v2

    Following the release of v1, Meta realized some limitations of v1:

    • uses React but only on the server side: no hydration nor Single-Page-App navigation
    • the repo was quite monolithic, difficult to maintain and test
    • not very extensible, there was no plugin system
    • the theming was relatively limited, and v1 sites look very similar

    In 2018, Meta decided to re-create Docusaurus from scratch, with a brand new architecture:

    • React used client and server side (like Next.js, Remix, Gatsby...)
    • Plugin system: allows the community to contribute useful features (OpenAPI integration, local search engine, Sass support...)
    • Much more flexible theming system: possible to override any React component of the theme
    • MDX (Markdown + React) to make your documentation interactive

    Docusaurus 2.0 has been in alpha/beta for 4 years 🤪, and we finally released the first stable version! We delayed this release because it wasn't obvious to respect Semantic Versioning before.

    Today, Docusaurus v2 is available with a lot of features, and it would be difficult to list them all: docs, blog, versioning, theming, dark mode, i18n, a11y, SEO, TypeScript, PWA, Algolia DocSearch, MDX...

    Who uses Docusaurus 2.0?

    A lot of companies have already adopted Docusaurus 2.0 despite the fact that it is in beta. The v2 is already much more popular than the v1, and we have almost half of the npm downloads of Gatsby!

    Our site showcase already contains almost 300 sites.

    And it is far from being exhaustive since so many sites have not yet been added: Figma, Snapchat, Courier, 1Password...

    Not to mention the many internal sites of companies like Microsoft, LinkedIn, Shopify...

    Some examples:

    • Big tech: Microsoft, Snapchat, Shopify, LinkedIn, Meta, SAP, ByteDance, Lacework, Palo Alto Network...
    • Startups: Supabase, Ionic, Hasura, Figma, Courier, CodeSandbox, StackBlitz, Redis, Algolia, Hashnode...
    • OSS: React-Native, Redux, React-Navigation, Jest, Testing Library, Gulp, Babel, Lerna, pnpm, Electron, Tauri...
    • Web3: Iota, Solana, Cardano, Neo, SushiSwap...

    We also have a lot of sites with beautiful customizations that really respect the brand image of the company.

    My role in all this?

    Two years ago, I was scrolling on Twitter when I came across this:

    Pro tip: join Twitter and be open 😄 It can lead to interesting career opportunities!

    I thought it was a great opportunity: working for Facebook as a freelancer on an open-source project doesn't happen every day. It was supposed to last 3 months, but it ended up lasting much longer: I've been working on Docusaurus for more than 2 years now!

    I have the chance to work in a very flexible way, with great autonomy, which also allows me to run this newsletter on the side.

    It's really cool to see Docusaurus becoming popular, I really feel like my work has a real impact. I hope I've inspired you to try this tool and I can't wait to see the sites you create! Don't forget to send them to our site showcase!

    Help us

    I would really appreciate if you could help us make the launch a success. An upvote, retweet, or comment helps us a lot!

    The main platforms we are launching on:

    • Twitter
    • Product Hunt
    • Hacker News

    Thanks and see you soon with some fresh React news :)

    Our cutest mascot Slash 💕

    Cristian Fernando - Jul 21

    Once suspended, sebastienlorber will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, sebastienlorber will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by sebastienlorber will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If sebastienlorber is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 使用 Python 部署 Azure 静态 Web 应用 | Deploy Azure Static Web Apps using Python
item.description:
现代静态站点（通常称为JAMstack 应用程序）以很少甚至免费的方式提供自定义 Web 体验。

我已经在自己的静态站点生成器上工作了 5 年。 随着时间的推移，它已经发展了很多，但有一件事是一致的。 这是现代蟒蛇！

许多人使用过github pages 、 Netlify或Vercel等工具，自从加入 Microsoft 以来，我想了解更多关于如何使用 Python 和Azure制作静态和动态网站的信息。

在团队庆祝产品发布一周年的第一周，我第一次了解了静态 Web 应用程序 (SWA)。 按照设计，该计划是通过混合 HTML 和可选的通过 Azure 函数的动态内容来提供 Web 应用程序。

可悲的是，与使用 Javascript 相比，您确实需要做更多的事情。

设置 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序（VS Code 方式）

您可以使用Azure 静态 Web 应用入门使用 VS Code 设置 Azure 静态 Web 应用。 为 Web 框架提供了一些选项，最接近您的将是 vanilla JS 路由。 我们将跳过前几个步骤，因为我们没有使用演示 repo（或 Javascript）。 我不想重新散列教程； 这是一个快速回顾：

  1. 在 VS Code 中打开项目的仓库
  2. 安装 Azure 扩展并登录到您的 Azure 帐户
  3. 在“资源”选项卡中选择 Azure 侧栏菜单
  4. 选择创建图标（加号）
  5. 在提示中，选择Create Static Web App
  6. 运行向导
    1. 连接您的 GitHub 帐户
    2. 为您的项目创建名称
    3. 选择您的首选地区

下一步是事情开始不同的地方。

  1. 重要事项选择CUSTOM作为部署方法。
  2. 将您的应用程序位置设置为项目的“/”路径或 Python 代码所在的任何位置。
  3. 将输出路径设置为您的代码将创建 HTML 的位置。

让您的网站启动并运行

遗憾的是，Azure 静态 Web 应用程序不支持轻松运行基于 python 的构建命令，但根据文档，我们只需要一个文件夹中的HTML文件来构建站点。 我们可以用几种不同的方式给它那个文件。 在哪里构建 HTML 将取决于您。

在镜像中构建

您不需要太多了解它的工作原理，但它会查找特定文件并根据它们选择构建规范。 如果你有requirements.txt ，Oryx 会知道使用 Python。 使用 Oryx 有一些妥协。 您可以通过将PYTHON_VERSION: "3.9.7"添加到 yaml 文件来更新到 Python 3.9.7。 此外，您需要将构建步骤添加到PRE_BUILD_COMMAND 2 。 您可以创建 shell 脚本或添加用&&分隔的步骤。

 - name : Build And Deploy id : builddeploy uses : Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1 with : azure_static_web_apps_api_token : ${{ secrets.AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN_MY_PROJECT }} repo_token : ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # Used for Github integrations (ie PR comments) action : " upload" ###### Repository/Build Configurations - These values can be configured to match your app requirements. ###### # For more information regarding Static Web App workflow configurations, please visit: https://aka.ms/swaworkflowconfig app_location : " /" # App source code path output_location : " OUTPUT_PATH" # Built app content directory - optional env : PYTHON_VERSION : 3.9.7 PRE_BUILD_COMMAND : ' pip --upgrade pip && pip install -r requirements.txt && python routes.py' # This can be a shell script as well

在镜像之前构建

您可以将构建步骤与部署的 Azure 映像分开，在 Github Actions 中构建它。

要在 GH 操作中构建您的环境，您需要在 Azure Build and Deploy部分之前向您的 yaml 文件添加一个块。 您需要包含setup-python操作并指定要使用的 python 版本。 使用 python 版本的主要版本，所以3.10而不是3.10.5 。 有关这方面的更多信息和选项，您可以查看Setup-Python GH 操作 repo 。

接下来，您需要添加运行步骤。 给这个部分起一个新名称并输入要运行的命令。

 run : az_build.sh # My run script is routes.py

对于多个命令，使用|-在开头，每个命令都在自己的行中。

我认为在性能方面没有太大差异。 你的代码在做同样的事情，只是在不同的地方。 使用此方法绕过 Oryx 的 Python 版本限制（或构建中的单独关注点）。

在推送到 Github 之前构建

还有另一种方法。 在本地构建您的站点并将其内容推送到 GithHub。

您将需要手动运行构建步骤。 最大的优点是完全控制，你不必修改你的 yaml 文件。

这样做的好处是，如果您进行精细更改，则不必重建整个站点。

查看您的网站

将代码推送到 GitHub 之后。 您将能够实时看到您的网站。 如果您不确定 URL，请检查 GitHub Actions Build，它会在构建步骤中告诉您。

您还可以刷新 VS Code 的 Azure 部分，它应该是可见的。

运行后，您可以在 Azure 门户中添加自定义域。

  1. 遗憾的是，我认为这不适用于使用 Pipenv 或 Poetry 的 Azure 静态 Web 应用程序。 您还可以将这些步骤添加到POST_BUILD_COMMAND 。 ↩ ↩



原文:

TLDR: Connect your repo to Azure Static Web Apps and use GitHub Actions to build your site via Python.

Modern Static Sites (popularly referred to as JAMstack Apps) provide a custom web experience for little to no cost.

I've been working on my own static site generator for going on 5 years now. It's developed a lot over time, but one thing is consistent. It's modern python!

Many folks have used tools like github pages, Netlify or Vercel, since joining Microsoft, I wanted to learn more about how to make both static and dynamic sites using Python and Azure.

I first learned about Static Web Apps (SWA) in my first week when the team was celebrating one year of the product. By design, the plan was to serve web applications by mixing HTML and optionally dynamic content via Azure functions.

Python has fallen out of favor for building static web apps, but it is still possible to deploy a static web app. Sadly, you do have to do a little more than if you were going to use Javascript.

Setup Azure Static Web Apps (The VS Code way)

You can setup Azure Static Web Apps with VS Code using the Azure Static Web Apps Getting Started. There provides a few options for web frameworks and the closest for you will be the vanilla JS route. We'll skip the first couple steps since we're not using the demo repo (or Javascript). I don't want to re-hash the tutorial; here is a quick recap:

  1. Open your project's repo in VS Code
  2. Install the Azure Extension and log in to your Azure account
  3. Select the Azure Side bar menu in the RESOURCES tab
  4. Select the create icon (plus sign)
  5. In the prompt, choose Create Static Web App
  6. Run through the wizard
    1. connecting your GitHub account
    2. creating a name for your project
    3. choose your preffered region

The next step is where things begin to differ.

  1. IMPORTANT Selecting CUSTOM as the Deployment Method.
  2. Set your app location to "/" path of your project or wherever your Python code lives.
  3. Set the output path to the location where your code will create HTML.

Getting your site up and running

Sadly, Azure Static Web Apps don't support running python based build commands easily, but according to the docs, we just need ONE HTML file in a folder to build a site. we can give it that file in a few different ways. Where you build your HTML will be up to you.

Build in the image

Azure uses a system called Oryx. You don't need to know too much about how it works but it looks for specific files and chooses build specs based on them. If you have a requirements.txt, Oryx will know to use Python. 1

Using Oryx comes with a few compromises. Oryx uses a Python 3.8 build by default. You can update up to Python 3.9.7 by adding PYTHON_VERSION: "3.9.7" to the yaml file. Also, you will need to add your build steps to the PRE_BUILD_COMMAND2. You can create shell script or add the steps seperated with &&.

- name: Build And Deploy id: builddeploy uses: Azure/static-web-apps-deploy@v1 with: azure_static_web_apps_api_token: ${{ secrets.AZURE_STATIC_WEB_APPS_API_TOKEN_MY_PROJECT }} repo_token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} # Used for Github integrations (i.e. PR comments) action: "upload" ###### Repository/Build Configurations - These values can be configured to match your app requirements. ###### # For more information regarding Static Web App workflow configurations, please visit: https://aka.ms/swaworkflowconfig app_location: "/" # App source code path output_location: "OUTPUT_PATH" # Built app content directory - optional  env: PYTHON_VERSION: 3.9.7 PRE_BUILD_COMMAND: 'pip --upgrade pip && pip install -r requirements.txt && python routes.py' # This can be a shell script as well

Build Before the Image

You can separate your build steps from the Azure image deployed, building it in Github Actions.

To build your environment in GH Actions you'll need to add a block to your yaml file BEFORE the Azure Build and Deploy section. You'll need to include the setup-python action and specify the python version you would like to use. Use the major version of your python version so 3.10 and NOT 3.10.5. For more information and options on this you can check out the Setup-Python GH actions repo.

Next you'll need to add the run steps. Give this section a new name and enter the commands to run.

 run: az_build.sh # My run script is routes.py

For more than one command, use |- at the beginning which each command being on its own line.

I don't think there is much of a difference performance-wise. You're code is doing the same thing, just in a different place. Use this method to bypass Python version limits of Oryx (or separate concerns in your build).

Build Before Pushing to Github

There is another way. Build your site locally and push its contents to GithHub.

You will need to manually run your build steps. The biggest advantages is complete control and you don't have to modify your yaml file.

The advantage of doing this is if you do granular changes, you don't have to rebuild the entire site.

Viewing your Site

After you push your code to GitHub. You'll be able to see your site live. If you aren't sure of the URL, check the GitHub Actions Build and it will tell you in the build steps.

You can also refresh the Azure Section of VS Code and it should be visible.

Once it's running you can add a custom domain in the Azure Portal.

  1. Sadly I don't think this works in Azure Static Web Apps using Pipenv or Poetry. ↩

  2. You can add also add those steps to the POST_BUILD_COMMAND. ↩
item.author: kjaymiller

桑吉特·莫汉蒂

Sanjit Mohanty

Posted on Aug 3

I have been writing & sharing my thoughts around APIs & API Gateways for quite sometime now.

Please do check out the following links on those -

Few months ago, I captured the current landscape of API Gateways in the industry. Link to it is here - https://sanjimoh.medium.com/api-gateway-landscape-347b91639f0b

Post that I started evaluating many of those API Gateways indicated over the above landscape.

I plan to release a series of videos & in each of these episodes, I will pick up one API Gateway and take you all through its capabilities, provisioning along with a demo & in the end share my parting thoughts.

My focus would be mostly around Kubernetes platform and how these API Gateways perform on it.
